Submodules¶

core.admin module¶

Admin configuration for the News Portal application.

This module registers models with the Django admin interface and customizes their display.

class core.admin.ArticleAdmin(model, admin_site)¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin view for Article model.

list_display = ('title', 'author', 'publisher', 'approved', 'created_at')¶
list_filter = ('approved', 'publisher', 'created_at')¶
property media¶
search_fields = ('title', 'content')¶
class core.admin.CustomUserAdmin(model, admin_site)¶

Bases: UserAdmin

Custom admin configuration for User model.

add_fieldsets = ((None, {'classes': ('wide',), 'fields': ('username', 'usable_password', 'password1', 'password2')}), ('Role and Subscriptions', {'fields': ('role', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists')}))¶
fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), ('Role and Subscriptions', {'fields': ('role', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists')}))¶
list_display = ('username', 'email', 'role', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser')¶
property media¶
model¶

alias of User

class core.admin.NewsletterAdmin(model, admin_site)¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin view for Newsletter model.

list_display = ('title', 'author', 'created_at')¶
property media¶
search_fields = ('title', 'description')¶
class core.admin.PublisherAdmin(model, admin_site)¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin view for Publisher model.

list_display = ('name',)¶
property media¶
search_fields = ('name',)¶

core.api_urls module¶

core.api_views module¶

class core.api_views.ArticleCreateAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: CreateAPIView

Protected API endpoint that allows journalists to create articles.

A valid token is required.

authentication_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication'>]¶
perform_create(serializer)¶

Save the current authenticated journalist as the article author.

permission_classes = [<class 'core.permissions.IsJournalist'>]¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class core.api_views.ArticleDeleteAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: DestroyAPIView

Protected API endpoint that allows editors and journalists to delete articles.

authentication_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication'>]¶
permission_classes = [<class 'core.permissions.IsEditorOrJournalist'>]¶
queryset¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class core.api_views.ArticleDetailAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: RetrieveAPIView

Public API endpoint that returns a single approved article.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.AllowAny'>]¶
queryset¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class core.api_views.ArticleListAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: ListAPIView

Public API endpoint that returns all approved articles.

No authentication is required for this endpoint.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.AllowAny'>]¶
queryset¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class core.api_views.ArticleUpdateAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: UpdateAPIView

Protected API endpoint that allows editors and journalists to update articles.

authentication_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication'>]¶
permission_classes = [<class 'core.permissions.IsEditorOrJournalist'>]¶
queryset¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class core.api_views.SubscribedArticleListAPIView(**kwargs)¶

Bases: ListAPIView

Authenticated API endpoint that returns approved articles from:

  • publishers the user is subscribed to

  • journalists the user is subscribed to

A valid token is required to access this endpoint.

authentication_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.authentication.TokenAuthentication'>]¶
get_queryset()¶

Build the queryset for subscribed content.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class core.apps.CoreConfig(app_name, app_module)¶

Bases: AppConfig

default_auto_field = 'django.db.models.BigAutoField'¶
name = 'core'¶
ready()¶

Override this method in subclasses to run code when Django starts.

core.forms module¶

property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class core.forms.RegisterForm(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: UserCreationForm

Form used to register a new user.

The role field comes from the custom User model, so once the model includes ‘publisher’ in ROLE_CHOICES, it will also appear in the registration form automatically.

class Meta¶

Bases: object

fields = ['username', 'email', 'role', 'password1', 'password2']¶
model¶

alias of User

base_fields = {'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.TypedChoiceField object>, 'username': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
declared_fields = {'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

core.models module¶

Models for the News Portal application.

This module defines all database models used in the system, including users, publishers, articles, and newsletters.

class core.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: Model

Represents a news article created by a journalist.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

approved¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

author¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id¶
content¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
id¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

newsletters¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>¶
publisher¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

publisher_id¶
title¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class core.models.User(*args, **kwargs)¶

Bases: AbstractUser

Custom user model with role-based access.

Roles: - reader: can view content and subscribe - editor: can approve articles - journalist: can create content

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

ROLE_CHOICES = [('reader', 'Reader'), ('editor', 'Editor'), ('journalist', 'Journalist')]¶
articles¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

auth_token¶

Accessor to the related object on the reverse side of a one-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Restaurant(Model):
    place = OneToOneField(Place, related_name='restaurant')

Place.restaurant is a ReverseOneToOneDescriptor instance.

class core.permissions.IsEditorOrJournalist¶

Bases: BasePermission

has_permission(request, view)¶

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

Bases: BasePermission

has_permission(request, view)¶

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class core.permissions.IsReaderOrReadOnly¶

Bases: BasePermission

has_permission(request, view)¶

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

core.signals module¶

core.signals.assign_user_to_group(sender, instance, created, **kwargs)¶
core.signals.create_roles_and_permissions(sender, **kwargs)¶

core.tests module¶

class core.tests.NewsPortalTests(methodName='runTest')¶

Bases: TestCase

Automated tests for the news portal application.

These tests cover: - role-based access - article visibility - journalist article creation - editor approval - newsletter creation - API endpoints - API authentication - subscribed article filtering

setUp()¶

Create test users, publisher, and sample articles used across tests.

test_api_journalist_can_create_article()¶

Journalists should be able to create articles through the API.

test_api_reader_cannot_create_article()¶

Readers should not be able to create articles through the API.

test_api_returns_only_approved_articles()¶

The public article list API should return approved articles only.

test_api_subscribed_requires_authentication()¶

The subscribed articles API should require token authentication.

test_api_subscribed_returns_only_subscribed_content()¶

The subscribed articles API should return only articles from the reader’s subscribed publishers and journalists.

test_dashboard_redirects_by_role()¶

Users should be redirected to the correct dashboard/page based on role.

test_editor_can_approve_article(mock_send_mail, mock_requests_post)¶

Editors should be able to approve articles.

Approval should: - set approved to True - trigger email logic - trigger external POST request logic

test_journalist_can_create_article()¶

Journalists should be able to create articles. New articles must be unapproved by default.

test_journalist_can_create_newsletter()¶

Journalists should be able to create newsletters linked to articles.

test_journalist_cannot_access_editor_review_page()¶

Journalists should not be able to access the editor review page.

test_reader_can_only_view_approved_articles()¶

Readers should only see approved articles in the article list.

test_reader_cannot_create_article()¶

Readers should not be able to create articles.

test_reader_cannot_view_unapproved_article_detail()¶

Readers should not be able to open the detail page for unapproved articles.

core.urls module¶

core.views module¶

Views for the News Portal application.

Handles user authentication, dashboards, article management, newsletter management, and subscription features.

core.views.approve_article_view(request, article_id)¶

Allow an editor to approve an article.

Once approved: - the article becomes visible to readers - subscriber email logic is triggered - a simple external API call is triggered

core.views.article_detail_view(request, article_id)¶

Show the details of a single article.

Readers and publishers can only open approved articles. Editors and journalists can open any article.

core.views.article_list_view(request)¶

Show articles and newsletters for the current user.

  • Readers can see approved articles and all newsletters.

  • Publishers can also see approved articles and newsletters.

  • Editors and journalists can see all articles and all newsletters.

  • Readers can subscribe to publishers and journalists from this page.

core.views.create_article_view(request)¶

Allow a journalist to create a new article.

New articles are unapproved by default and must be reviewed by an editor.

core.views.create_newsletter_view(request)¶

Allow journalists to create newsletters.

core.views.create_publisher_view(request)¶

Allow editors to create publisher records.

This is separate from user registration.

core.views.dashboard_view(request)¶

Redirect users to the correct page based on their role.

  • editor -> editor review page

  • journalist -> journalist dashboard

  • reader -> article list page

  • publisher -> newsletter list page

core.views.delete_article_view(request, article_id)¶

Allow journalists to delete their own articles. Editors may delete any article.

core.views.delete_newsletter_view(request, newsletter_id)¶

Allow journalists to delete their own newsletters. Editors may delete any newsletter.

core.views.editor_article_list_view(request)¶

Show all articles and newsletters for editors.

Editors can review, approve, update, and delete content here.

core.views.home_view(request)¶

Show the landing page for users who are not logged in.

If a user is already authenticated, redirect them to the dashboard so the system can route them according to their role.

core.views.journalist_dashboard_view(request)¶

Show the journalist dashboard with the journalist’s own articles and newsletters.

core.views.login_view(request)¶

Log in a user with valid credentials.

core.views.logout_view(request)¶

Log out the current user and redirect to the login page.

core.views.newsletter_detail_view(request, newsletter_id)¶

Show the details of a single newsletter and its linked articles.

core.views.newsletter_list_view(request)¶

Show the newsletter feed.

This allows readers and publishers to access newsletters created by journalists.

core.views.register_view(request)¶

Register a new user and log them in immediately after successful signup.

core.views.subscribe_journalist_view(request, journalist_id)¶

Allow readers to subscribe to journalists.

core.views.subscribe_publisher_view(request, publisher_id)¶

Allow readers to subscribe to publishers.

core.views.unsubscribe_journalist_view(request, journalist_id)¶

Allow readers to unsubscribe from journalists.

core.views.unsubscribe_publisher_view(request, publisher_id)¶

Allow readers to unsubscribe from publishers.

core.views.update_article_view(request, article_id)¶

Allow journalists to update their own articles. Editors may update any article.

core.views.update_newsletter_view(request, newsletter_id)¶

Allow journalists to update their own newsletters. Editors may also update any newsletter.

Module contents¶

News_Portal_Capstone

Navigation

Related Topics

  • Documentation overview
